channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Luigi Mangione, alleged UnitedHealthcare CEO killer, charged with murder in New York
item.description: Luigi Mangione, 26, has been charged with murder in New York City in connection with the brazen shooting death of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson on Dec. 4.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing charged with murder in New York, court records show
item.description: Court records show New York prosecutors have charged a man with murder in the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak And Mark Scolforo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US defense secretary in Japan to discuss regional security as US military pauses Osprey flights
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — U.S. Secretary of Defense Lloyd Austin is in Japan on Tuesday to meet with Japanese officials and reaffirm the importance of their alliance and the U.S. commitment to regional security as threats rise from China and North Korea.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Descendant of last native leader of Alaska island demands Japanese reparations for 1942 invasion
item.description: The great-granddaughter of the last Alaska Native chief of a remote island in the Bering Sea believes Japan should pay more restitution for what its soldiers did to the residents of Attu Island during World War II.
item.dc:creator: Mark Thiessen And Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Family of Luigi Mangione speaks out following arrest and arraignment
item.description: The family of Luigi Mangione, the 26-year-old man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in New York City, has released a statement following his arrest on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KREX 5

item.title: Video shows thieves speed off in sports cars from Michigan dealership
item.description: A group of suspected car thieves sped away from a Michigan dealership in rare sports vehicles, leading police on a high-speed chase that ended with the arrest of a 19-year-old Detroit woman.
item.dc:creator: Duncan MacLean, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Party trays, meal kits sold in Utah recalled over cucumber-related Salmonella concerns
item.description: Multiple companies have issued recalls of products sold across the nation and in Utah after potential exposure to Salmonella through now-recalled cucumbers.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Biden creates Native American boarding school national monument to mark era of forced assimilation
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — President Joe Biden designated a national monument at a former Native American boarding school in Pennsylvania on Monday to honor the resilience of Indigenous tribes whose children were forced to attend the school and hundreds of similar abusive institutions.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House from 'Home Alone' is for sale — but it's not Kevin McCallister's house
item.description: The property, which recently hit the market in Winnetka, Illinois, was featured in the 1990 holiday classic "Home Alone" — but it wasn't the grand McAllister residence.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO murder suspect arrested at Pennsylvania McDonald's
item.description: UPenn graduate Luigi Mangione, 26, had a manifesto criticizing the healthcare industry when he was taken into questioning in connection with Brian Thompson's murder.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jay-Z accused of sexually assaulting 13-year-old in 2000 with Sean 'Diddy' Combs
item.description: Jay-Z said his only heartbreak is for his family, as having to explain the allegations will be hard on the Carter-Knowles' three children.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: TikTok asks federal appeals court to bar enforcement of potential ban until Supreme Court review
item.description: TikTok asked a federal appeals court on Monday to bar the Biden administration from enforcing a law that could lead to a ban on the popular platform until the Supreme Court reviews its challenge to the statute.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing charged with murder, court records show
item.description: A man in Pennsylvania has been arrested in connection with the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cracker Barrel 'refused service' to students with disabilities, Maryland school says
item.description: Cracker Barrel has issued an apology after a Maryland school district said a group of students with disabilities was denied service at one of the chain's restaurants.
item.dc:creator: Skyler Sales, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Polygamist with 20 'wives' to be sentenced for sex acts with children
item.description: A polygamous religious leader who claimed more than 20 spiritual “wives ” including 10 underage girls faces decades in prison at his sentencing on Monday for coercing girls as young as 9 years old to submit to criminal sex acts with him and other adults.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO assassination: Former police chief confident killer will be caught
item.description: A former high-ranking police chief says he is confident that the various law enforcement agencies on the hunt for UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson’s killer will eventually find him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Social Security beneficiaries at risk of delays amid GOP funding fight
item.description: Social Security beneficiaries could face longer wait times for service next year unless Congress agrees to the White House budget office’s request to increase funding for the Social Security Administration (SSA), the administration that runs the program is warning.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dodgers reach $17M, 1-year deal with Conforto and $22M, 2-year contract with Treinen, AP sources say
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The Los Angeles Dodgers agreed to a $17 million, one-year contract to add outfielder Michael Conforto and a $22 million, two-year deal to retain right-hander Blake Treinen, people familiar with the negotiations told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Woman, 52, accused of robbing Billie Eilish fans at knifepoint near Oregon arena
item.description: A woman was arrested for allegedly stabbing a man, then threatening and robbing a couple of their sleeping bag near the Moda Center Saturday morning.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, KTLA 5

item.title: Jay-Z accused of raping 13-year-old in 2000 with Diddy
item.description: The amended complaint identifies Jay-Z as "Celebrity A," who was accused of raping the 13-year-old while "Celebrity B," a female, watched.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: 10 hospitalized after motorcycle officer crashes into crowd at California parade
item.description: A crash involving a motorcycle officer left at least 10 people hospitalized during a holiday parade in Palm Springs, California, Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he can't guarantee tariffs won't raise US prices and won't rule out revenge prosecutions
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump said he can't guarantee that his promised tariffs on key U.S. foreign trade partners won't raise prices for American consumers and he suggested once more that some political rivals and federal officials who pursued legal cases against him should be imprisoned.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Big Ten wins playoff selection derby, followed by SEC despite notable Alabama omission
item.description: College football's conference shakeup left concerns about two super conferences dominating the playoff field. They weren't totally unfounded or 100% borne out. The Big Ten, not the Southeastern Conference, was the biggest winner Sunday. The ACC scored, too.
item.dc:creator: JOHN ZENOR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Netflix must pay $385K to woman exposed as fertility doctor's secret child: court
item.description: A federal court in Indianapolis has ordered Netflix to pay $385,000 to an Indiana woman it outed as the daughter of Donald Cline, a former fertility doctor who secretly used his own sperm to inseminate patients.
item.dc:creator: Austin Hanson, Fox 5

item.title: 5 recipes from the pros to give after-dinner cocktails some holiday flair
item.description: From a festive Old Fashioned to a boozy hot chocolate, these after-dinner drinks might inspire your own menu of holiday cocktails, to be sipped slowly by the fire or washed down with your Christmas pudding.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why work-life balance isn't working for women
item.description: About half of working women reported feeling stressed “a lot of the day," compared to about 4 in 10 men, according to a Gallup report published this week.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ousted Syrian leader Assad flees to Moscow, Russian state media say
item.description: Syrians poured into streets echoing with celebratory gunfire on Sunday after a stunning rebel advance reached the capital, putting an end to the Assad family's 50 years of iron rule but raising questions about the future of the country and the wider region.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: LA Galaxy strike early, hold off New York Red Bulls 2-1 to win their record 6th MLS Cup championship
item.description: CARSON, Calif. (AP) — The LA Galaxy finished 26th in the 29-team Major League Soccer standings just one season ago, and their biggest supporters boycotted certain matches to protest a decade of poor performance.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Days after gunman killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO, police push to ID him and FBI offers reward
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The gunman who killed the CEO of the largest U.S. health insurer likely left New York City on a bus soon after the brazen ambush that has shaken corporate America, police officials said. But he left something behind: a backpack that was discovered in Central Park.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police find bag carried by gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO, say he likely fled NYC on bus
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The gunman who killed the CEO of the largest U.S. health insurer likely left New York City on a bus soon after the brazen ambush that has shaken corporate America, police officials said Friday. But he left something behind: a backpack that was discovered in Central Park.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about Northern California's rare tsunami warning
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Northern California was under a rare and brief tsunami warning alert Thursday that tested local emergency notification systems after a 7.0 magnitude earthquake shook part of the state.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Crews recover the body of a woman from a Pennsylvania sinkhole after a 4-day search
item.description: The remains of a woman who fell into a sinkhole were recovered Friday, four days after she went missing while searching for her cat, a state police spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Officials release bodycam video of police shooting that killed 15-year-old Ohio boy
item.description: AKRON, Ohio (AP) — The mayor and police chief in an Ohio city where a 15-year-old boy was fatally shot by police on Thanksgiving night said bodycam video of the shooting raises questions that must be addressed. The boy's family said Friday that they want answers, too.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Indianapolis police officers are acquitted in the 2022 death of a man at his parents’ home
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Two Indianapolis police officers were acquitted of manslaughter and other charges Friday in the death of a man after officers shocked him with a Taser and restrained him face down while handcuffing him.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former national security officials urge caution on Gabbard
item.description: A group of nearly 100 former national security officials urged the Senate to “carefully scrutinize” former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), President-elect Trump’s nominee to serve as director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Wind-driven brushfire at Baltimore wood recycling yard closes expressway, light rail, schools
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Firefighters were battling a massive, wind-driven brushfire at a Baltimore wood recycling yard that closed a section of the expressway into the city’s downtown, the neighboring light rail line and nearby roads and schools on Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bitcoin hits $100,000 after Trump names pro-crypto SEC chair
item.description: The former president is bolstering the evolving platform amidst the threat of new currency systems seeking to challenge the U.S. dollar.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9
